title: What to know about mucus in throat before using medicinal herbs
intro: Many herbal teas effectively alleviate mucus in the throat, and three of the best include ginger, eucalyptus, and slippery elm. Ginger tea, made from the root of the ginger plant, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and mucus. Eucalyptus tea, derived from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, acts as a natural expectorant, loosening and expelling mucus from the throat. Lastly, slippery elm tea, extracted from the inner bark of the slippery elm tree, soothes irritation and creates a protective barrier in the throat, reducing mucus production. These herbal teas offer a natural, caffeine-free, and side-effect-free solution to alleviate mucus in the throat.
definition: Mucus in the throat, also known as phlegm, is a slippery substance produced by the respiratory system. It plays a crucial role in protecting the body by trapping dust, germs, and other foreign particles that enter the body through the nose or mouth. Mucus contains enzymes and antibodies that help destroy or neutralize harmful substances, preventing them from causing damage to the lungs. Its constant production and elimination are essential for maintaining respiratory health and overall well-being.
